scientific_question: Can the live slow Agent autonomously author an admissible single-surface edit that changes fast-Agent behavior, improves a remaining repairable target scope, survives the automatic risk set, is promoted, and is then reused on held-out cases?

phase_1.early_stop: Do not run cycle 1 when cycle 0 produces no Harness promotion; diagnose and repair the exact failed link first.

phase_2.condition: cycle 0 promotes at least one SUPPORTED_EDIT
phase_2.action: Run a separately preregistered held-out reuse slice with new seed, location, or severity from the promoted snapshot.

primary_success.description: The live proposal-to-replay path remains open under the corrected contracts.

m0_growth_success.description: This is required before declaring the live M0 growth loop closed.

family_expectations.impulsive_outlier: Primary unsaturated repairable family; public robust-z evidence separates current targets from genuine-event risks, but the Agent must author the capability itself.
family_expectations.level_shift: Not a growth target in this run because corrected domain-naive H0 already achieves positive repair on all six calibration targets.
family_expectations.missing: Interpret against actual damage and probe evidence; identity is correct when no reliable positive repair signal exists.
family_expectations.period_change: Correct identity is Agent-level success while OPERATOR_GAP remains a separate system backlog item.

causal_replay: target recovery fraction and median gain

anti_redefinition_rules: A schema-valid proposal without replay is not Harness growth.
An outcome gain without the predicted behavior is UNEXPECTED_GAIN and cannot be promoted.
Level-shift absolute success cannot be counted as causal edit gain after H0 saturation was observed.
Reference wind-tunnel edits are calibration assets and cannot be counted as autonomous growth.
If cycle 0 has no promotion, cycle 1 is not an informative reuse test and must not be run.
